top1pct_removed.interpretation: Removing top 1% (22 extreme observations) flips mean from +0.36% to -0.04%. The positive gross mean is entirely manufactured by ~1% of observations.

top5pct_removed.interpretation: After removing top 5% (114 obs), mean approaches zero (-0.007%). CI width collapses but remains zero-crossing. No signal survives.

winsorization.interpretation: Winsorized mean (cap at 5th/95th percentile) = +0.017%. CI [-0.12, 0.14] crosses zero. Winsorized distribution shows no edge.

trimmed_means.interpretation: 5% trim removes gross mean from 0.36% to 0.01%. 10% trim → 0.007%. Progressive trimming confirms CLV is tail-driven noise.

outlier_concentration.interpretation: Top 1% of observations (by absolute value) contribute 110.57% of total CLV sum — i.e., all other 99% of observations are net negative. The overall positive mean is a concentration artifact of extreme outliers. This is a textbook outlier-dominated distribution.

distributional_analysis.interpretation: The central 90% of observations lie within [-6.7, +6.8]. The 10% tails drive all signal. With 27.7% neutral observations, the distribution is three-mass: zero-locked / small-movement / extreme-outlier.

sensitivity_verdict: The CLV signal DOES NOT survive ANY outlier robustness check. Gross mean collapses from +0.36% to ≤+0.017% under all three removal methods (trim/winsorize/exclusion). Mean sign flips to negative when top 1% removed. Classification: INCONCLUSIVE — outlier dominated, not a stable edge.
